body_txt: Drums are primordial instruments, among the first humans devised. They also aren't generally used to play "definite pitch," required to make a melody. Therefore is sometimes assumed that drummers do not need to be smart to play them. But here, it is assumed that drummers do not need to be civilized or even tame . The drummer of the band is often depicted as a caveman who furiously attacks his instruments with Primitive Clubs that would destroy a cello. He may be The Berserker channeling unlimited aggression into a frenzy of noise. Maybe he becomes a Human Hummingbird , swinging so fast and frantically it is hard to see what he's doing, but impressive beats emanate from within the Big Ball of Violence . If the setting allows it, he can be a genuine Beast Man or Funny Animal . Note that savage manners do not necessarily diminish the drummer's performance, and may indeed enhance it if a frenzied sound is what the band wants. Compare Bunny-Ears Lawyer for other quirky but talented characters. For more on the contrast between passion and discipline, see Technician Versus Performer . Maybe this drummer is a Temperate Berserker : beastly energy is limited to performance on-stage, and he is much calmer behind the scenes ... but if not, his bandmates (and anyone else in his personal orbit) may have to deal with constant chaos. At best, this drummer is famous as the tireless Life of the Party or The Prankster . At worst, he is infamous as an insufferable Jerkass or out-of-control Manchild . Darker variations portray him as an Undiscriminating Addict whose animalistic behavior is the result of Drug-Crazed Savagery . This is technically true (humans are animals), and sometimes Truth in Television , but not always. Contrary to another trope , drumming demands remarkable intelligence, since much of it is improvisational and more complex than traditional compositions, especially in genres like jazz. Drums also require the largest and most muscular movements of any instrument in a modern/rock music repertoire, so (regardless of personality) it really helps to be physically fit. Therefore, a Real Life percussionist is more likely to be a Genius Lightning Bruiser than The Brute . When dealing with a Five-Man Band , The Big Guy usually becomes the drummer. If the Team Pet is an Intellectual Animal , they can also fill this role. Compare Cymbal-Banging Monkey , Dumb and Drummer (with which this can sometimes overlap; however, a wild drummer is not necessarily stupid and vice versa), and Rotten Rock & Roll . Also compare Primal Chest-Pound , where a character drums on oneself , also often indicating a savage, animalistic personality. See also Nobody Loves the Bassist for another example of a band-member stereotype, and Rockers Smash Guitars for other rock musicians causing mayhem.
